allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking gives emergence the whole-system frame needed to see the interrelationships that produce new behavior.
allies.source_quote: Systems thinking is a discipline for seeing wholes and seeing interrelationships rather than things.

allies.rationale_text: Synthesis-and-integration helps emergence turn scattered interaction findings into a coherent higher-level pattern.
allies.source_quote: The process of creating a core message involves taking elementary findings and clustering them until they cohere into a higher-level synthesis.

antagonists.rationale_text: Optimization-theory can mislead emergence when local objective maximization suppresses weak signals that only appear at system level.
antagonists.source_quote: emergence conflicts with optimization-theory (optimization theory) when fixed objective functions suppress weak signals that reveal new system behavior.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Emergence conflicts with first-principles-thinking when reductive decomposition misses interaction effects that only appear once the system is recombined.
structured_tensions.source_quote: emergence conflicts with first-principles-thinking (first principles thinking) when reductive decomposition misses interaction effects that appear only at system level.

curation_notes.summary: Preserved the strongest whole-system ally, one synthesis ally, and the explicit reduction-versus-interaction tension block.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader design-thinking and analogy sprawl beyond the clearest current-vocabulary seams.
open_questions: Additional allies are source-real, but the first-order surface is cleanest around systems-thinking and synthesis-and-integration.
